title: Implement server ShootC2SPacket handler + fire rate control + modifier version anti-cheat

reference_files.reason: Step 2: Fire rate control - MinecraftServer.getTickCount(), interval=max(1,round(20/fire_rate)), fire_rate<=0 no shooting, TPS drop reduces actual fire rate proportionally
Modifier version anti-cheat - per-player inconsistency counter + baseline snapshot, consistent->reset, inconsistent->counter+1, counter>=3 -> cheat (reject+WARN+reset), counter<3 -> tolerate
Server-side processing flow - validate gun, validate modifierVersion, fire rate control, read look angle + spread, read attributes + snapshot, register bullet, broadcast

acceptance_criteria: ShootPacketHandler created to receive ShootC2SPacket on server side
Step 1 validation: verify player's main-hand item is gun (ModularShootAPI.isGun), reject if not
Fire rate control: read fire_rate from player's attribute (getAttributeValue(ModularShootAttributes.FIRE_RATE))
fire_rate <= 0: reject shooting (interval treated as infinite, avoid division by zero)
fire_rate > 0: interval = max(1, round(20 / fire_rate)) ticks
Check if MinecraftServer.getTickCount() - lastShootTick >= interval, reject if not reached (packet discarded, no makeup)
Per-gun lastShootTick tracking (Map<UUID player, Map<ResourceLocation gunId, Integer lastTick>> or similar)
Modifier version anti-cheat: per-player inconsistency counter (initial 0) + baseline snapshot
Packet version == server current version: counter reset to 0, allow shooting, update baseline

Packet version != server version: counter +1, if counter < 3 tolerate (use server version, update baseline, counter not reset), if counter >= 3 flag cheat (reject + WARN log with player name + versions + reset counter + reset baseline)
If all checks pass: delegate to shooting engine orchestrator (subtask 17) for steps 3-9
Handler methods kept under 50 lines each (split validation, fire rate, anti-cheat into separate methods)
